title: AGENTS for Rfmalloc
text: Rfmalloc R package providing file-backed ALTREP vector types powered bundled fmalloc allocator runtime. package supports persistent scratch runtimes, ALTREP serialization, explicit native/R destroy path via destroy_fmalloc_vector().

title: AGENTS for Rfmalloc
text: making package changes, load package-development guidance r-package-development: /root/.pi/agent/git/github.com/sounkou-bioinfo/pi-skills/skills/r-package-development/SKILL.md. Follow repo’s existing conventions R/, src/, man/, inst/tinytest/.

title: AGENTS for Rfmalloc
text: Keep newest entries top ## Development (unreleased) (latest section). Add concise, user-visible bullets grouped feature/fix area. Prefer one bullet per behavior change (e.g. persistence/unserialize semantics, destroy/recovery behavior, diagnostics). edit historical release notes; append new work top section.

code: @Manual{,   title = {Rfmalloc: Persistent ALTREP Vectors},   author = {Sounkou Mahamane Toure},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/sounkou-bioinfo/Rfmalloc}, }

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: Rfmalloc experimental R package file-backed R vectors using patched copy fmalloc. current R-facing vector API creates fmalloc-backed ALTREP vectors atomic, character, list storage. Earlier prototypes explored R’s custom allocator / Rf_allocVector3() path (see Simon Urbanek’s proof--concept). package moved direct ALTREP vectors public vector API control copy--write duplication. patched fmalloc malloc/free/realloc layer still built installed native consumers use C API link bundled library.

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: Implemented now: fmalloc-backed ALTREP vectors logical, integer, numeric, raw, complex, character, list values; list elements restricted NULL Rfmalloc vectors runtime; explicit runtime handles persistent / scratch runtime modes; persistent serialization reopening fixed-width atomic character vectors; fmalloc-backed coercion subset-copy results supported vector types; nested list-container recovery reference persistent runtimes; explicit destroy_fmalloc_vector() immediate vector invalidation/cleanup parent-reference safety checks; -file persistent allocation catalog used validate serialized references; installed C header R_RegisterCCallable() API packages. Known limitations: Core Ops, Summary, Math, Math2 workflows dispatched S3 methods common vector/matrix paths. rowSums(), colSums(), rowMeans(), colMeans() fall back base implementations (warning) input exact 2D matrix dims != 1L. Scalar zero-length outputs Summary, Math, Math2 (example sum(x) returning single value) ordinary R scalars design. Full operator- method-family coverage still incomplete; additional edge cases may still materialize ordinary R objects. Still experimental: richer catalog inspection tooling, mapped-file recovery diagnostics.

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("sounkou-bioinfo/Rfmalloc")

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: examples use temporary backing files. Several chunks wrap code local() .exit() can clean files README rendered; required normal interactive use. code, keep runtime handle need call cleanup_fmalloc() finished. shortest form uses init_fmalloc(), opens backing file stores package default runtime. Calls create_fmalloc_vector() can omit runtime argument: multiple backing files explicit lifetime management, prefer runtime handles returned open_fmalloc():
code: library(Rfmalloc) local({   alloc_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   init_fmalloc(alloc_file)   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc()     unlink(alloc_file)   }, add = TRUE)    v_int <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 10)   v_num <- create_fmalloc_vector("numeric", 10)   v_raw <- create_fmalloc_vector("raw", 4)   v_cplx <- create_fmalloc_vector("complex", 4)   v_chr <- create_fmalloc_vector("character", 3)   v_lst <- create_fmalloc_vector("list", 2)    v_int[1:3] <- c(1L, 2L, 3L)   v_num[1:3] <- c(1.1, 2.2, 3.3)   v_raw[] <- as.raw(1:4)   v_cplx[] <- c(1+1i, 2+2i, 3+3i, 4+4i)   v_chr[] <- c("alpha", "beta", NA_character_)    list_child <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 2)   list_child[] <- 1:2   v_lst[[1]] <- list_child    list(     integer = v_int[1:3],     numeric = v_num[1:3],     raw = v_raw[],     complex = v_cplx[],     character = v_chr[],     list_first = v_lst[[1]][]   ) }) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e9594d45dd.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> $integer #> [1] 1 2 3 #>  #> $numeric #> [1] 1.1 2.2 3.3 #>  #> $raw #> [1] 01 02 03 04 #>  #> $complex #> [1] 1+1i 2+2i 3+3i 4+4i #>  #> $character #> [1] "alpha" "beta"  NA      #>  #> $list_first #> [1] 1 2 local({   handle_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(handle_file)   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(handle_file)   }, add = TRUE)    v <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 10, runtime = rt)   v[1:3] <- 10:12   v[1:3] }) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e989f3425.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> [1] 10 11 12

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: example executed building README uses larger (still modest) fmalloc payload demonstration.
code: local({   large_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(large_file)   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(large_file)   }, add = TRUE)    n <- 2e6   before_alloc <- nrow(list_fmalloc_allocations(rt))    big_int_a <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", n, runtime = rt)   big_int_b <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", n, runtime = rt)   big_int_a[1:5] <- 1:5   big_int_b[1:5] <- 6:10    # Elementwise arithmetic stays on fmalloc-managed ALTREP for the full-length result   big_sum <- big_int_a + big_int_b   after_alloc <- nrow(list_fmalloc_allocations(rt))    list(     a_head = big_int_a[1:5],     b_head = big_int_b[1:5],     sum_head = big_sum[1:5],     sum_length = length(big_sum),     sum_managed = inherits(big_sum, "fmalloc") &&       grepl("fmalloc_altrep", capture.output(.Internal(inspect(big_sum)))[[1L]], fixed = TRUE),     allocations_delta = after_alloc - before_alloc   )    # sum(big_sum) is not used here: Summary(S) generics return scalars. }) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e944c9ef49.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> Creating fmalloc ALTREP vector: type=integer, length=2000000 #> Large allocation: 7.63 MB requested #> SUCCESS: fmalloc allocated 8000000 bytes #> Successfully created fmalloc ALTREP vector #> Creating fmalloc ALTREP vector: type=integer, length=2000000 #> Large allocation: 7.63 MB requested #> SUCCESS: fmalloc allocated 8000000 bytes #> Successfully created fmalloc ALTREP vector #> Creating fmalloc ALTREP vector: type=integer, length=2000000 #> Large allocation: 7.63 MB requested #> SUCCESS: fmalloc allocated 8000000 bytes #> Successfully created fmalloc ALTREP vector #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> $a_head #> [1] 1 2 3 4 5 #>  #> $b_head #> [1]  6  7  8  9 10 #>  #> $sum_head #> [1]  7  9 11 13 15 #>  #> $sum_length #> [1] 2000000 #>  #> $sum_managed #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $allocations_delta #> [1] 3

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: destroy_fmalloc_vector() immediately releases native bookkeeping one fmalloc ALTREP vector returns logical indicating whether live vector destroyed. helper enforces parent-reference safety: vector destroyed still stored child fmalloc list. needed, drop parent links first. persistent mode, destroying default semantics retains payload bytes recorded -disk allocation remains recoverable normal serialize() flows. Use destroy_fmalloc_vector(x, unsafe = TRUE) reclaim persistent payload memory mark catalog entry non-recoverable. scoped targeted vector(s): one object unsafe-destroyed, objects runtime remain recoverable.
code: local({   destroy_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(destroy_file, mode = "persistent")   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(destroy_file)   }, add = TRUE)    child <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 2, runtime = rt)   child[] <- 1:2    parent <- create_fmalloc_vector("list", 1, runtime = rt)   parent[[1]] <- child    destroy_error <- try(destroy_fmalloc_vector(child), silent = TRUE)    parent[[1]] <- NULL   destroy_ok <- destroy_fmalloc_vector(child)    list(     destroy_rejected_with_parent = inherits(destroy_error, "try-error"),     destroy_succeeded_after_unset = destroy_ok   ) }) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e96f8d6ca2.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> $destroy_rejected_with_parent #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $destroy_succeeded_after_unset #> [1] TRUE local({   selective_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(selective_file, mode = "persistent")   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(selective_file)   }, add = TRUE)    keep <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 3, runtime = rt)   drop <- create_fmalloc_vector("character", 2, runtime = rt)   keep[] <- c(101L, 202L, 303L)   drop[] <- c("alpha", "beta")    keep_blob <- serialize(keep, NULL)   drop_blob <- serialize(drop, NULL)    destroy_fmalloc_vector(drop, unsafe = TRUE)    keep_recovered <- unserialize(keep_blob)   drop_recover_error <- try(unserialize(drop_blob), silent = TRUE)    list(     keep_recovered_ok = all.equal(keep_recovered[], c(101L, 202L, 303L)) == TRUE,     drop_recover_fails = inherits(drop_recover_error, "try-error")   ) }) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e942621e08.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> Using existing file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e942621e08.bin (size: 33562624 bytes) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e942621e08.bin (init: false, mode: persistent) #> Using existing file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e942621e08.bin (size: 33562624 bytes) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e942621e08.bin (init: false, mode: persistent) #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> $keep_recovered_ok #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $drop_recover_fails #> [1] TRUE

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: open_fmalloc() init_fmalloc() accept mode = "persistent" mode = "scratch". default "persistent". persistent mode, committed vector payloads returned fmalloc vector finalizers. Fixed-width atomic vectors serialize reference physical allocation backing file. scratch mode, backing file temporary allocation arena. Vector finalizers may return payloads fmalloc, serialization falls back ordinary R values.
code: local({   persist_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(persist_file, mode = "persistent")   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(persist_file)   }, add = TRUE)    x <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 5, runtime = rt)   x[] <- 1:5   roundtrip <- unserialize(serialize(x, NULL))   roundtrip[] }) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e9390801c7.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> Using existing file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e9390801c7.bin (size: 33562624 bytes) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e9390801c7.bin (init: false, mode: persistent) #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> [1] 1 2 3 4 5

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: R’s internal inspector dispatches ALTREP Inspect hook. Rfmalloc’s inspector reports vector type, length, payload byte count, runtime mode, runtime state, payload offset, file UUID, backing-file path. inspect() output internal R diagnostic, exact formatting can vary R versions. fmalloc_altrep ... line comes Rfmalloc’s ALTREP Inspect method.
code: local({   inspect_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(inspect_file, mode = "persistent")   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(inspect_file)   }, add = TRUE)    inspect_vec <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 4, runtime = rt)   inspect_vec[] <- 1:4   .Internal(inspect(inspect_vec)) }) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e91879d164.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> @5c7128fa7c78 13 INTSXP g0c0 [OBJ,REF(1),ATT] fmalloc_altrep type=integer length=4 bytes=16 data=0x79fdd0c023e8 mode=persistent runtime=open offset=9192 uuid=4959906f00552fc8ef5cffcbcc14b1b3 file=/tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e91879d164.bin #> ATTRIB: #>   @5c7128fa6c48 02 LISTSXP g0c0 [REF(1)]  #>     TAG: @5c71252466a0 01 SYMSXP g1c0 [MARK,REF(37017),LCK,gp=0x6000] "class" (has value) #>     @5c71291437d8 16 STRSXP g0c3 [REF(65535)] (len=3, tl=0) #>       @5c712a5ea3b8 09 CHARSXP g1c2 [MARK,REF(30),gp=0x60] [ASCII] [cached] "fmalloc_vector" #>       @5c712a0af6c8 09 CHARSXP g1c1 [MARK,REF(95),gp=0x60] [ASCII] [cached] "fmalloc" #>       @5c7125279658 09 CHARSXP g1c1 [MARK,REF(520),gp=0x61] [ASCII] [cached] "integer" #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: Rfmalloc character vectors ALTSTRING vectors. String bytes, lengths, encodings, NA flags live fmalloc storage. R CHARSXP values materialized STRING_ELT() access; Rfmalloc allocate CHARSXP objects inside fmalloc.
code: local({   char_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(char_file, mode = "persistent")   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(char_file)   }, add = TRUE)    chars <- create_fmalloc_vector("character", 3, runtime = rt)   chars[] <- c("one", NA_character_, "three")    from_int <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 3, runtime = rt)   from_int[] <- 1:3    list(     chars = chars[],     from_integer = as.character(from_int)[]   ) }) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e932a9c2bd.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> $chars #> [1] "one"   NA      "three" #>  #> $from_integer #> [1] "1" "2" "3"

what: Matrix and data.frame constructors and converters
title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: Use constructor helpers shape-aware allocation, as_fmalloc_*() helpers install shape metadata existing fmalloc-backed vectors:
code: local({   ctor_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(ctor_file, mode = "persistent")   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(ctor_file)   }, add = TRUE)    m <- create_fmalloc_matrix("integer", nrow = 2L, ncol = 3L, runtime = rt)   m[] <- 1:6    base_vec <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 6L, runtime = rt)   base_vec[] <- 11:16   m_view <- as_fmalloc_matrix(base_vec, ncol = 3L, copy = FALSE)    # Metadata-only reshape, then mutate through the reshaped object   m_view[1L, 2L] <- 88L    a <- create_fmalloc_array("numeric", dim = c(2L, 1L, 3L), runtime = rt)   a[] <- 1:6    col_a <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 3L, runtime = rt)   col_b <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 3L, runtime = rt)   col_a[] <- c(1L, 2L, 3L)   col_b[] <- c(4L, 5L, 6L)   df <- as_fmalloc_data_frame(a = col_a, b = col_b, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)    list(     matrix_dims = dim(m),     metadata_shares_payload = c(base_vec[3L], m_view[1L, 2L]),     array_dims = dim(a),     df_columns = names(df)   ) }) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e940b1ada6.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> $matrix_dims #> [1] 2 3 #>  #> $metadata_shares_payload #> [1] 13 88 #>  #> $array_dims #> [1] 2 1 3 #>  #> $df_columns #> [1] "a" "b"

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: rowSums(), colSums(), rowMeans(), colMeans(), Summary pathways keep small results regular R objects default avoid unnecessary small temporary mappings. can tune behavior : options(Rfmalloc.reduce_result_length = n) n maximum allowed result length keep -memory. Results length greater n stay fmalloc-backed.
code: local({   reduce_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(reduce_file, mode = "persistent")   old_limit <- getOption("Rfmalloc.reduce_result_length")   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     options(Rfmalloc.reduce_result_length = old_limit)     unlink(reduce_file)   }, add = TRUE)    m <- create_fmalloc_matrix("integer", nrow = 4L, ncol = 4L, runtime = rt)   m[] <- 1:16    base_m <- matrix(1:16, nrow = 4L, ncol = 4L)   tiny_vec <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 4L, runtime = rt)   tiny_vec[] <- 11:14    options(Rfmalloc.reduce_result_length = 5L)   default_row_sums <- rowSums(m)   default_range <- range(tiny_vec)    options(Rfmalloc.reduce_result_length = 3L)   compact_row_sums <- rowSums(m)    options(Rfmalloc.reduce_result_length = 1L)   compact_range <- range(tiny_vec)    list(     default_row_sums_in_memory = inherits(default_row_sums, "fmalloc"),     compact_row_sums_filebacked = inherits(compact_row_sums, "fmalloc"),     default_range_in_memory = inherits(default_range, "fmalloc"),     compact_range_filebacked = inherits(compact_range, "fmalloc"),     rowSums_values = unclass(compact_row_sums),     expected_rowSums = rowSums(base_m)   ) }) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e9607f4e7c.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> $default_row_sums_in_memory #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $compact_row_sums_filebacked #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $default_range_in_memory #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $compact_range_filebacked #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $rowSums_values #> [1] 28 32 36 40 #>  #> $expected_rowSums #> [1] 28 32 36 40

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: Runtime handles make possible use one backing file one R process. Calling cleanup_fmalloc(rt_a) marks rt_a closed. vectors rt_a still reachable, native mapping kept alive vectors garbage-collected. implemented native live-vector count ALTREP-held external pointer fmalloc-backed vector; user-visible attribute added vector.
code: local({   file_a <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   file_b <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")    rt_a <- open_fmalloc(file_a)   rt_b <- open_fmalloc(file_b, size_gb = 0.1)    vec_a <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 10, runtime = rt_a)   vec_b <- create_fmalloc_vector("numeric", 10, runtime = rt_b)   vec_a[1] <- 101L   vec_b[1] <- 202    before_cleanup <- data.frame(     vector = c("vec_a", "vec_b"),     value = c(vec_a[1], vec_b[1])   )    cleanup_fmalloc(rt_a)   after_cleanup <- vec_a[1]   cleanup_fmalloc(rt_b)   unlink(c(file_a, file_b))    list(     before_cleanup = before_cleanup,     vec_a_after_cleanup = after_cleanup   ) }) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e9671072d3.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> Requested file size: 0.10 GB (107374182 bytes) #> Creating file with size: 107374182 bytes (0.10 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e928868430.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> $before_cleanup #>   vector value #> 1  vec_a   101 #> 2  vec_b   202 #>  #> $vec_a_after_cleanup #> [1] 101

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: Rfmalloc ALTREP serialization path persistent runtimes. persistent fixed-width atomic character vectors, serialize() stores small reference record instead copying vector payload serialization stream. record contains: format tag: "RfmallocRef"; reference format version; backing-file path; backing-file UUID; R vector type; vector length; payload offset inside mapped file; payload byte size. unserialize(), Rfmalloc reopens backing file, verifies UUID, checks recorded dimensions file bounds, validates catalog record generation, reconstructs ALTREP vector around fmalloc allocation.
code: local({   ser_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(ser_file, mode = "persistent")    ints <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 4, runtime = rt)   ints[] <- 101:104   chars <- create_fmalloc_vector("character", 3, runtime = rt)   chars[] <- c("alpha", NA_character_, "gamma")    catalog <- list_fmalloc_allocations(rt)   ints_blob <- serialize(ints, NULL)   chars_blob <- serialize(chars, NULL)    cleanup_fmalloc(rt)    ints_recovered <- unserialize(ints_blob)   chars_recovered <- unserialize(chars_blob)   output <- list(     catalog = catalog[, c("record_offset", "generation", "type", "length")],     integers = ints_recovered[],     characters = chars_recovered[]   )    unlink(ser_file)   output }) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e93125b855.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> Using existing file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e93125b855.bin (size: 33562624 bytes) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e93125b855.bin (init: false, mode: persistent) #> Using existing file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e93125b855.bin (size: 33562624 bytes) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e93125b855.bin (init: false, mode: persistent) #> $catalog #>   record_offset generation      type length #> 1          9440          2 character      3 #> 2          9224          1   integer      4 #>  #> $integers #> [1] 101 102 103 104 #>  #> $characters #> [1] "alpha" NA      "gamma"

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: Use diagnose_fmalloc_runtime() inspect runtime metadata, live catalog, compact summary can help decide runtime reset candidate: Scratch runtimes use ordinary R serialization instead. serialized values depend reopening scratch backing file. Rfmalloc list vectors intentionally general R lists. Element assignment accepts NULL Rfmalloc-backed vectors runtime; ordinary R objects base vectors, data frames, arbitrary lists rejected. keeps lists inside file-backed object universe. persistent runtimes, list containers now serialize reference state children recoverable fmalloc vectors. serialized state stores per-slot child descriptors, nested list containers can reopen recursively backing file without carrying session-local SEXP pointers. reference-based recovery, name-based object discovery. catalog stores physical allocation records, user variable names. Serialized references use catalog record offset generation validation; catalog can listed, yet high-level object store recovering vectors name.
code: local({   diag_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(diag_file, mode = "persistent")    a <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 4, runtime = rt)   b <- create_fmalloc_vector("character", 2, runtime = rt)   a[] <- 1:4   b[] <- c("left", "right")    pre <- diagnose_fmalloc_runtime(rt)   destroy_fmalloc_vector(a)   post <- diagnose_fmalloc_runtime(rt)    list(     mode = post$runtime$mode,     pre_records = pre$summary$record_count,     pre_committed = pre$summary$committed_records,     post_records = post$summary$record_count,     post_tombstones = post$summary$tombstoned_records,     compaction_implemented = post$summary$compaction_implemented   ) }) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e975efa79f.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> $mode #> [1] "persistent" #>  #> $pre_records #> [1] 2 #>  #> $pre_committed #> [1] 2 #>  #> $post_records #> [1] 2 #>  #> $post_tombstones #> [1] 1 #>  #> $compaction_implemented #> [1] FALSE local({   scratch_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   scratch_rt <- open_fmalloc(scratch_file, mode = "scratch")    scratch_vec <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 4, runtime = scratch_rt)   scratch_vec[] <- 1:4   scratch_copy <- unserialize(serialize(scratch_vec, NULL))    cleanup_fmalloc(scratch_rt)   unlink(scratch_file)   scratch_copy }) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e9657d1441.bin (init: true, mode: scratch) #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> [1] 1 2 3 4 #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "fmalloc_vector" "fmalloc"        "integer"

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: List assignment rejects non-fmalloc payloads runtime. Nested fmalloc lists recover recursively serialized unserialized persistent backing file. Cross-runtime insertion also rejected.
code: local({   reject_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(reject_file, mode = "persistent")   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(reject_file)   }, add = TRUE)    fm_list <- create_fmalloc_vector("list", 2, runtime = rt)   child <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 2, runtime = rt)   child[] <- c(1L, 2L)   fm_list[[1]] <- child    bad_assignment <- try({     fm_list[[2]] <- 1:2   }, silent = TRUE)    list(     same_runtime_child = fm_list[[1]][],     rejected_non_fmalloc_payload = inherits(bad_assignment, "try-error")   ) }) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e927df324a.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> $same_runtime_child #> [1] 1 2 #>  #> $rejected_non_fmalloc_payload #> [1] TRUE local({   recover_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(recover_file, mode = "persistent")   on.exit(unlink(recover_file), add = TRUE)    child <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 2, runtime = rt)   child[] <- 1:2    nested <- create_fmalloc_vector("list", 1, runtime = rt)   nested[[1]] <- child    labels <- create_fmalloc_vector("character", 2, runtime = rt)   labels[] <- c("alpha", "beta")    fm_list <- create_fmalloc_vector("list", 2, runtime = rt)   fm_list[[1]] <- nested   fm_list[[2]] <- labels    blob <- serialize(fm_list, NULL)    cleanup_fmalloc(rt)    recovered <- unserialize(blob)   list(     recovered_nested = recovered[[1]][[1]][],     recovered_labels = recovered[[2]]   ) }) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e960e73411.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> Using existing file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e960e73411.bin (size: 33562624 bytes) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e960e73411.bin (init: false, mode: persistent) #> $recovered_nested #> [1] 1 2 #>  #> $recovered_labels #> [1] "alpha" "beta" local({   cross_file_a <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   cross_file_b <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")    rt_a <- open_fmalloc(cross_file_a, mode = "persistent")   rt_b <- open_fmalloc(cross_file_b, mode = "persistent")   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt_a)     cleanup_fmalloc(rt_b)     unlink(c(cross_file_a, cross_file_b))   }, add = TRUE)    child_other_runtime <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 2, runtime = rt_b)   child_other_runtime[] <- c(10L, 20L)    fm_list <- create_fmalloc_vector("list", 1, runtime = rt_a)   cross_runtime_error <- try({     fm_list[[1]] <- child_other_runtime   }, silent = TRUE)    list(cross_runtime_rejected = inherits(cross_runtime_error, "try-error")) }) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e979610575.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e9241b2f61.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> $cross_runtime_rejected #> [1] TRUE

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: Rfmalloc vectors regular R vectors R’s point view, different operations exercise different paths R ALTREP: vectorized sequential operations sum(x) can use contiguous payload access; scalar loops use ALTREP element access call-heavy; x[] creates fmalloc-backed subset copy supported vector types; bench::mark() reports R heap allocation mem_alloc; count bytes stored fmalloc mapped file. small benchmark uses bench scratch runtime, benchmark-fmalloc allocations preserved persistent records. Timings machine- R-version-specific; use template local measurements.
code: perf_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin") rt <- open_fmalloc(perf_file, mode = "scratch", size_gb = 0.1) #> Requested file size: 0.10 GB (107374182 bytes) #> Creating file with size: 107374182 bytes (0.10 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp7SgtRU/file1780e96ebc80c.bin (init: true, mode: scratch)  n <- 100000L set.seed(1) idx <- sample.int(n, 2000L)  base_int <- integer(n) base_int[] <- seq_len(n)  fm_int <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", n, runtime = rt) #> Creating fmalloc ALTREP vector: type=integer, length=100000 #> Successfully created fmalloc ALTREP vector fm_int[] <- base_int  write_env <- new.env(parent = emptyenv()) write_env$base <- base_int write_env$fm <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", n, runtime = rt) #> Creating fmalloc ALTREP vector: type=integer, length=100000 #> Successfully created fmalloc ALTREP vector write_env$fm[] <- base_int  scalar_sum <- function(x, i) {   total <- 0L   for (j in i) total <- total + x[[j]]   total }  perf_result <- bench::mark(   base_sequential_sum = sum(base_int),   fmalloc_sequential_sum = sum(fm_int),   base_scalar_read = scalar_sum(base_int, idx),   fmalloc_scalar_read = scalar_sum(fm_int, idx),   base_subset_copy = base_int[idx],   fmalloc_subset_copy = fm_int[idx],   base_indexed_write = {     write_env$base[idx] <- 0L     invisible(write_env$base[1L])   },   fmalloc_indexed_write = {     write_env$fm[idx] <- 0L     invisible(write_env$fm[1L])   },   iterations = 20,   check = FALSE )[, c("expression", "median", "itr/sec", "mem_alloc", "gc/sec")]  rm(fm_int, write_env) invisible(gc()) cleanup_fmalloc(rt) #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up unlink(perf_file) perf_result #> # A tibble: 8 × 5 #>   expression               median `itr/sec` mem_alloc `gc/sec` #>   <bch:expr>             <bch:tm>     <dbl> <bch:byt>    <dbl> #> 1 base_sequential_sum     32.13µs    29912.        0B        0 #> 2 fmalloc_sequential_sum   44.1µs    24119.        0B        0 #> 3 base_scalar_read        30.04µs    30271.   24.55KB        0 #> 4 fmalloc_scalar_read     702.7µs     1418.        0B        0 #> 5 base_subset_copy         2.42µs   347957.    7.86KB        0 #> 6 fmalloc_subset_copy      7.49µs   121146.        0B        0 #> 7 base_indexed_write      79.07µs    13545.  390.67KB        0 #> 8 fmalloc_indexed_write  179.09µs     5738.        0B        0

what: Native C API for Other Packages
title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: Rfmalloc installs inst/include/Rfmalloc.h registers C-callable entry points R_RegisterCCallable(). Downstream packages can add Rfmalloc LinkingTo Imports, include header, use inline wrappers. current native surface exposes runtime open/cleanup, vector creation, default-runtime lookup, catalog listing, runtime diagnostics, API-version query. Returned SEXP objects follow normal R API ownership rules.

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: fmalloc: https://github.com/yasukata/fmalloc Simon Urbanek’s Rf_allocVector3() custom mmap allocator PoC: https://gist.github.com/s-u/6712c97ca74181f5a1a5 Rfmalloc’s prior custom allocator implementation using Rf_allocVector3() (historical comparison): https://github.com/sounkou-bioinfo/Rfmalloc/commit/0165953

what: Rfmalloc: Memory-Mapped File Allocation for R — Rfmalloc-package
title: Rfmalloc: Memory-Mapped File Allocation for R — Rfmalloc-package
text: Rfmalloc provides experimental memory-mapped file allocation capabilities R using patched copy fmalloc library. current package exposes ALTREP file-backed vector allocation logical, integer, numeric, raw, complex, character, list vectors fmalloc payload storage.

title: Rfmalloc: Memory-Mapped File Allocation for R — Rfmalloc-package
text: open_fmalloc Open explicit fmalloc runtime handle. init_fmalloc Open install default fmalloc runtime. create_fmalloc_vector Create vectors using fmalloc. create_fmalloc_matrix Create matrix-shaped fmalloc vectors. create_fmalloc_array Create array-shaped fmalloc vectors. create_fmalloc_data_frame Create data.frames fmalloc-backed columns. as_fmalloc_matrix Convert fmalloc vectors matrix-shaped objects. as_fmalloc_array Convert fmalloc vectors array-shaped objects. as_fmalloc_data_frame Convert fmalloc-backed objects data.frame. list_fmalloc_allocations List persistent allocation catalog records. diagnose_fmalloc_runtime Summarize persistent allocation catalog state runtime diagnostics. cleanup_fmalloc Request cleanup fmalloc runtime.

title: Rfmalloc: Memory-Mapped File Allocation for R — Rfmalloc-package
text: ALTREP file-backed allocation logical, integer, numeric, raw, complex, character, list vectors. List elements restricted NULL Rfmalloc-backed vectors runtime. Large allocations spanning multiple fmalloc chunks. Multiple runtime handles one R process. Persistent scratch runtime modes. Reference serialization persistent fixed-width atomic character ALTREP vectors. Fmalloc-backed ALTREP subset copies vector indexing operations. -file allocation catalog persistent vectors. C-callable API installed header packages. Native lifetime tracking runtime mappings outlive reachable vectors allocated . Runtime catalog diagnostics planning recovery operational cleanup.

title: Rfmalloc: Memory-Mapped File Allocation for R — Rfmalloc-package
text: ALTREP-backed dispatch now covers core Ops, Summary, Math, Math2, matrix rowSums/colSums/rowMeans/colMeans workflows S3 methods common vector/matrix usage. Explicit base-fallback boundaries : rowSums(), colSums(), rowMeans(),           colMeans() fall back base implementations (warning)           input exact 2D matrix dims != 1L. Scalar zero-length outputs Summary, Math,           Math2 (example sum(x) returning single value)           ordinary R scalars design. Full operator- method-family coverage still incomplete R generics. advanced families may still materialize ordinary R objects edge cases.

title: Rfmalloc: Memory-Mapped File Allocation for R — Rfmalloc-package
text: Future work includes view-based subset representations, catalog compaction reset tooling, metadata storage attributes persisted elements, robust nested-list reference validation, compaction recovery metadata.

title: Rfmalloc: Memory-Mapped File Allocation for R — Rfmalloc-package
text: Maintainer: Sounkou Mahamane Toure sounkoutoure@gmail.com contributors: Kenichi Yasukata (fmalloc) [copyright holder] Wolfram Gloger (ptmalloc3) [copyright holder] Free Software Foundation, Inc. (selected GNU C Library support files) [copyright holder]

title: Create Vector Using fmalloc — create_fmalloc_vector
text: Creates ALTREP vector using file-backed fmalloc runtime. returned object ALTREP creation time. Fixed-width atomic payload bytes allocated directly fmalloc, ALTREP duplication vector subsetting keep copy--write copies fmalloc-backed without using R's non-API Rf_allocVector3() path.

title: Create Vector Using fmalloc — create_fmalloc_vector

title: Create Vector Using fmalloc — create_fmalloc_vector
text: type Character string specifying vector type. Supported values "logical", "integer", "numeric"/"double", "raw", "complex", "character", "list". Fixed-width atomic types expose direct writable fmalloc DATAPTR; character vectors store string bytes fmalloc materialize R CHARSXP values demand; list vectors use ALTREP element access R-visible reference sidecar GC safety accept NULL fmalloc-backed vectors runtime elements. Persistent list containers serialized nested reference states elements recoverable runtime. length Integer specifying non-negative length vector create. runtime Optional runtime handle returned open_fmalloc(). supplied, default runtime established init_fmalloc() used.

what: Explicitly destroy a fmalloc vector — destroy_fmalloc_vector
title: Explicitly destroy a fmalloc vector — destroy_fmalloc_vector
text: Releases runtime bookkeeping single fmalloc ALTREP vector immediately. scratch mode, payload memory immediately reclaimed. persistent mode, vector payload retained default existing -disk state remains durable; optional unsafe = TRUE reclaims payload memory marks metadata non-recoverable.

title: Explicitly destroy a fmalloc vector — destroy_fmalloc_vector
code: destroy_fmalloc_vector(x, unsafe = FALSE)

title: Explicitly destroy a fmalloc vector — destroy_fmalloc_vector
text: x Fmalloc ALTREP vector destroy. unsafe Whether physically free persistent payload bytes. Unsafe destroy intended short-lived scratch-like cleanup mark catalog entry non-recoverable.

title: Explicitly destroy a fmalloc vector — destroy_fmalloc_vector
text: Logical value indicating whether live vector destroyed.

title: Explicitly destroy a fmalloc vector — destroy_fmalloc_vector
text: Explicit destroy fails vector still referenced another fmalloc list vector child.

title: Diagnose fmalloc runtime state — diagnose_fmalloc_runtime

title: Diagnose fmalloc runtime state — diagnose_fmalloc_runtime
text: runtime Optional runtime handle returned open_fmalloc(). supplied, current default runtime used.

title: Diagnose fmalloc runtime state — diagnose_fmalloc_runtime
text: named list three components: runtime: runtime metadata file path, UUID, mode, catalog counters, live vectors, reference state; catalog: full allocation catalog returned list_fmalloc_allocations(); summary: compact set computed diagnostics explicit compaction status note.

title: Diagnose fmalloc runtime state — diagnose_fmalloc_runtime
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ rt <- open_fmalloc(tempfile(fileext = ".bin"), mode = "persistent") x <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 4, runtime = rt) y <- create_fmalloc_vector("logical", 2, runtime = rt) diagnose_fmalloc_runtime(rt) cleanup_fmalloc(rt) } # }

title: Initialize fmalloc — init_fmalloc
text: Compatibility wrapper opens fmalloc runtime installs package default runtime used create_fmalloc_vector() explicit runtime supplied.

title: Initialize fmalloc — init_fmalloc
code: init_fmalloc(filepath, size_gb = NULL, mode = c("persistent", "scratch"))

title: Initialize fmalloc — init_fmalloc
text: filepath Character string specifying file path fmalloc data. size_gb Numeric value specifying size backing file GB (optional). specified, uses package default size new files existing file size. mode Runtime mode. "persistent" keeps committed vector payloads backing file serializes fixed-width atomic vectors reference. "scratch" uses backing file large temporary allocation arena serializes vectors value.

title: Initialize fmalloc — init_fmalloc
text: Logical indicating whether file newly initialized.

title: Initialize fmalloc — init_fmalloc
text: new code, prefer open_fmalloc() pass returned runtime handle create_fmalloc_vector().

title: Initialize fmalloc — init_fmalloc
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ alloc_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin") init_fmalloc(alloc_file) v <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 1000) cleanup_fmalloc() unlink(alloc_file) } # }

title: List Persistent fmalloc Allocations — list_fmalloc_allocations
text: Returns -file allocation catalog persistent fmalloc runtime. catalog stored backing file records physical allocation metadata used validate serialized persistent references.

title: List Persistent fmalloc Allocations — list_fmalloc_allocations
code: list_fmalloc_allocations(runtime = NULL)

title: List Persistent fmalloc Allocations — list_fmalloc_allocations
text: runtime Optional runtime handle returned open_fmalloc(). supplied, default runtime established init_fmalloc() used.

title: List Persistent fmalloc Allocations — list_fmalloc_allocations
text: data frame one row per catalog record columns describing catalog record offset, generation, state, vector type, length, payload offset, payload byte size, flags, whether record recoverable reference serialization.

title: List Persistent fmalloc Allocations — list_fmalloc_allocations
text: successful recovery, look state column: "committed": valid serialized payload exists record; "tombstone": payload destroyed non-recoverable unless runtime remains open referenced directly existing SEXP; transient states internal generally expected. recoverable indicates whether record can reopened via serialized reference metadata. payload_offset == 0 payload_nbytes == 0 generally indicates non-payload entry.

title: List Persistent fmalloc Allocations — list_fmalloc_allocations
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ rt <- open_fmalloc(tempfile(fileext = ".bin")) v <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 10, runtime = rt) list_fmalloc_allocations(rt) cleanup_fmalloc(rt) } # }

title: Open an fmalloc Runtime — open_fmalloc
text: Opens file-backed fmalloc runtime returns external-pointer handle. Multiple runtime handles may open one R process. Runtime mode controls whether vector payloads durable persistent allocations scratch allocations can returned fmalloc ALTREP handles garbage-collected.

title: Open an fmalloc Runtime — open_fmalloc
code: open_fmalloc(filepath, size_gb = NULL, mode = c("persistent", "scratch"))

title: Open an fmalloc Runtime — open_fmalloc
text: filepath Character string specifying file path fmalloc data. size_gb Numeric value specifying size backing file GB (optional). specified, uses package default size new files existing file size. mode Runtime mode. "persistent" keeps committed vector payloads backing file serializes fixed-width atomic vectors reference. "scratch" uses backing file large temporary allocation arena serializes vectors value.

title: Open an fmalloc Runtime — open_fmalloc
text: external pointer class fmalloc_runtime.
